action.reasoning: Observation: iter_3 got 0.3512 but task_8 and task_9 are 0.0 accuracy because the 'best' checkpoint was saved at task 7 (mid-training). The final model after all 10 tasks had lower val acc (0.3210) so wasn't saved as best. Analysis: Critical bug - need to always save the final checkpoint after all tasks complete, not just the best mid-training one. Also, the DER++ approach needs improvement. Decision: Fix checkpoint saving (always save final), increase memory to 8000, increase epochs to 30, and use cosine LR schedule. Training time was ~150s for 20 epochs, so 30 epochs should take ~225s - still fast enough.
